metadata.description: Comprehensive halal/haram ingredient database for the Halal Check API
sources: IFANCA (Islamic Food and Nutrition Council of America)

ingredients.name: Gelatin
ingredients.reason: Commercial gelatin is overwhelmingly derived from pork skin, bones, or non-halal slaughtered cattle. Unless specifically certified as halal (bovine or fish gelatin with halal certification), it must be avoided.

ingredients.name: Lard
ingredients.reason: Fat from pigs. Explicitly haram in the Quran (Al-Baqarah 2:173, Al-Ma'idah 5:3, Al-An'am 6:145).

ingredients.name: Ethanol
ingredients.reason: Intoxicating alcohol is explicitly haram (Al-Ma'idah 5:90-91). Used in food, beverages, flavorings, and pharmaceuticals.

ingredients.name: Pepsin
ingredients.reason: An enzyme typically extracted from pig stomach lining. If microbial or plant-based, it is halal, but must be verified.

ingredients.name: Rennet (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Enzyme extracted from calf stomach. Haram if the calf was not slaughtered according to Islamic law. Microbial rennet and plant rennet are halal alternatives.

ingredients.name: Carmine
ingredients.reason: Red dye derived by crushing cochineal insects. Considered haram by many scholars because consuming insects is generally prohibited, though some Hanafi scholars permit it. JAKIM and MUI classify it as haram.

ingredients.name: Pork Derivatives
ingredients.reason: Any product derived from pigs is categorically haram, including meat, fat, skin, organs, and extracts.

ingredients.name: Wine and Wine Vinegar (from wine)
ingredients.reason: Wine is khamr (intoxicant) and haram. Wine vinegar derived from wine is a matter of scholarly dispute; some say it becomes halal through transformation (istihalah), but many major bodies (JAKIM, MUI) require halal certification.

ingredients.name: L-Cysteine (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Amino acid commonly derived from human hair or duck feathers in China. If derived from non-halal slaughtered animal sources, it is haram. Synthetic or microbial L-cysteine is halal.

ingredients.name: Albumin (egg - contaminated)
ingredients.reason: Egg-based albumin is halal. However, serum albumin from blood (especially porcine) is haram. Must verify the source. Many processed foods use egg albumin which is fine.

ingredients.name: Animal Shortening
ingredients.reason: Unless derived from halal-slaughtered cattle/sheep and certified, animal shortening is assumed to be from non-halal or mixed sources including pork.

ingredients.name: Pancreatin
ingredients.reason: Enzyme mixture from pork pancreas. Used in digestive supplements and food processing.

ingredients.name: Mono and Diglycerides (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Emulsifiers that may be derived from pork fat, non-halal animal fat, or plant sources (soy, palm). Must verify source. If plant-based, halal. If animal-based without halal certification, haram.

ingredients.name: Polysorbates (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: May contain oleic acid derived from pork or non-halal animal fat. Plant-based polysorbates are halal.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Extract
ingredients.reason: Contains ethanol as a solvent (typically 35% alcohol). Many scholars consider the small amount in food acceptable, but JAKIM and MUI require halal-certified alternatives. Vanilla flavor (synthetic) is typically halal.

ingredients.name: Shellac
ingredients.reason: Secreted by lac insects (Kerria lacca). Used as a glazing agent on candies and pills. Considered haram by JAKIM and MUI due to insect origin.

ingredients.name: Lard Oil
ingredients.reason: Oil derived from pig fat. Used in frying, baking, and food manufacturing.

ingredients.name: Blood and Blood Products
ingredients.reason: Consuming blood is explicitly prohibited in Islam (Al-Ma'idah 5:3). Includes blood plasma, serum albumin, and any blood-derived ingredient.

ingredients.name: Dogs (any derivative)
ingredients.reason: Dogs are najis (impure) in Islamic law. Any product derived from dogs is haram.

ingredients.name: Lecithin (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Lecithin labeled as soy lecithin or sunflower lecithin is halal. If the source is not specified, it may be from egg or animal. Most commercial lecithin is from soy (halal). Egg lecithin is halal.

ingredients.name: Stearic Acid (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from pork fat, beef fat, or plant sources (palm oil). Must verify source. Plant-derived stearic acid is halal.

ingredients.name: Glycerol / Glycerin (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Can be derived from animal fat (including pork), petroleum, or plant oils. Synthetic/vegetable glycerin is halal. Animal glycerin must be from halal-certified source.

ingredients.name: Cochineal
ingredients.reason: Red pigment from crushed cochineal insects. Same as carmine. Haram by JAKIM/MUI standards.

ingredients.name: Bone Char
ingredients.reason: Used to filter sugar and water. Typically derived from animal bones (often non-halal). Sugar filtered through bone char is considered haram by many scholars.

ingredients.name: Lipase (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Enzyme that may be from pig or calf pancreas. Used in cheese making and food processing. Microbial lipase is halal.

ingredients.name: Whey (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Byproduct of cheese making. If animal rennet was used in cheese production, whey is contaminated. Whey from microbial/vegetarian rennet or halal-certified cheese is halal.

ingredients.name: Casein / Caseinate (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Milk protein itself is halal, but may use non-halal rennet or enzymes in processing. Caseinate used as an emulsifier may also use non-halal processing aids.

ingredients.name: Rum
ingredients.reason: Alcoholic beverage. Any product containing rum or rum extract (which contains alcohol) is haram.

ingredients.name: Beer
ingredients.reason: Alcoholic beverage. All beer and beer-flavored products are haram. Non-alcoholic beer (0.0% ABV) is generally considered halal by JAKIM.

ingredients.name: Spirits / Liquor
ingredients.reason: All intoxicating liquors and spirits are haram regardless of source.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Alcohol Denatonium Benzoate
ingredients.reason: Contains ethanol. Even denatured alcohol is considered haram for consumption.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol
ingredients.reason: Synthetic compound. Not derived from animals. Commonly used as a food additive, solvent, and humectant. Halal-certified.

ingredients.name: Citric Acid
ingredients.reason: Primarily produced by fungal fermentation (Aspergillus niger). Not derived from animals. Widely accepted as halal.

ingredients.name: Ascorbic Acid
ingredients.reason: Vitamin C, typically produced by fermentation or synthesized. All major bodies confirm it is halal.

ingredients.name: Tocopherols (Vitamin E)
ingredients.reason: Can be extracted from vegetable oils (soy, sunflower - halal) or from gelatin capsules (haram if pork-based). The pure vitamin E from plant sources is halal. Verify carrier/capsule material.

ingredients.name: Niacin / Niacinamide
ingredients.reason: Synthetic vitamin. No animal derivatives. Halal-certified.

ingredients.name: Riboflavin (Vitamin B2)
ingredients.reason: Can be produced by fermentation or extracted from animal sources. If synthetic or from plant fermentation, halal. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Pectin
ingredients.reason: Extracted from fruit peels (apples, citrus). No animal derivatives. Halal.

ingredients.name: Agar-Agar
ingredients.reason: Plant-based gelling agent derived from red algae/seaweed. Halal alternative to gelatin.

ingredients.name: Carrageenan
ingredients.reason: Extracted from red seaweed (Irish moss). Plant-based thickener and gelling agent. Halal.

ingredients.name: Xanthan Gum
ingredients.reason: Produced by bacterial fermentation (Xanthomonas campestris). Not derived from animals. Halal.

ingredients.name: Guar Gum
ingredients.reason: Derived from guar beans (Cyamopsis tetragonoloba). Plant-based thickener. Halal.

ingredients.name: Locust Bean Gum
ingredients.reason: Derived from carob tree seeds. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Gum Arabic
ingredients.reason: Natural gum from Acacia tree sap. Plant-based. Halal. Widely used in Middle Eastern cuisine.

ingredients.name: Titanium Dioxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based white pigment. Not derived from animals. Halal. (Note: banned in EU as food additive since 2022 for health reasons, but halal status is confirmed.)

ingredients.name: Silicon Dioxide
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based anti-caking agent. Not derived from animals. Halal.

ingredients.name: Calcium Carbonate
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based. Not derived from animals (when from geological sources). Halal.

ingredients.name: Beeswax
ingredients.reason: Produced by honey bees. Honey is halal (mentioned in Quran, An-Nahl 16:69). Beeswax is a halal-approved product of bees.

ingredients.name: Honey
ingredients.reason: Explicitly mentioned in the Quran as healing (An-Nahl 16:69). All pure honey is halal.

ingredients.name: Sodium Benzoate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative. No animal derivatives. Halal.

ingredients.name: Potassium Sorbate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative derived from sorbic acid. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sorbic Acid
ingredients.reason: Can be extracted from rowan berries or produced synthetically. Both sources are halal.

ingredients.name: MSG
ingredients.reason: Produced by bacterial fermentation of starch/sugar. Not derived from animals. Halal.

ingredients.name: Yeast Extract
ingredients.reason: Derived from yeast (fungus), not animals. Used as a flavor enhancer. Halal. (Some yeast extracts in alcoholic beverages are processed with alcohol - those are haram.)

ingredients.name: Soy Sauce (regular)
ingredients.reason: Traditional soy sauce is brewed with wheat and soy and may contain small amounts of alcohol from fermentation. Japanese-style soy sauces often contain significant alcohol. Halal-certified soy sauce is available.

ingredients.name: Miso
ingredients.reason: Fermented soybean paste that may contain alcohol from fermentation process. Halal-certified miso is available.

ingredients.name: Fish Gelatin
ingredients.reason: Gelatin derived from fish skin/bones is halal (all seafood is halal in Islam). Must verify no cross-contamination with pork products.

ingredients.name: Bovine Gelatin (halal-certified)
ingredients.reason: Gelatin from cattle slaughtered according to Islamic law (zabiha). Must have halal certification.

ingredients.name: Collagen (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Bovine collagen must be from halal-slaughtered cattle. Porcine collagen is haram. Marine (fish) collagen is halal. Must verify source and certification.

ingredients.name: Lanolin
ingredients.reason: Extracted from sheep's wool. Generally considered halal, but must be from sheep slaughtered according to Islamic law or from live sheep shearing. Most commercial lanolin is from live sheep and acceptable.

ingredients.name: Oleic Acid (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Can be from plant (olive, sunflower - halal) or animal (pork/beef tallow) sources. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Palmitic Acid (non-halal)
ingredients.reason: Can be from palm oil (halal) or animal fat. Most commercial palmitic acid is from palm oil, but must verify.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Stearate
ingredients.reason: Used in pills and supplements as a lubricant. Source of stearic acid matters - can be plant or animal based. Plant-based is halal.

ingredients.name: Dicalcium Phosphate
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based. May be from rock phosphate or bone. Most commercial food-grade DCP is from mineral sources. Halal-certified.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Bicarbonate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic leavening agent. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Baking Soda
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based leavening agent. Halal.

ingredients.name: Cream of Tartar
ingredients.reason: Byproduct of winemaking. Most scholars consider it halal due to chemical transformation (istihalah) as it no longer contains alcohol. JAKIM certifies it as halal.

ingredients.name: Sucralose
ingredients.reason: Artificial sweetener derived from sugar. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Aspartame
ingredients.reason: Artificial sweetener. Synthesized from amino acids. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Stevia
ingredients.reason: Natural sweetener from Stevia rebaudiana plant. Halal.

ingredients.name: Cane Sugar (filtered)
ingredients.reason: Some white sugar is filtered through bone char. Beet sugar is never filtered through bone char. Organic sugar and most European sugar avoid bone char. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Annatto
ingredients.reason: Natural food coloring from the seeds of the achiote tree. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Turmeric
ingredients.reason: Natural spice and coloring from Curcuma longa plant. Halal.

ingredients.name: Beetroot Red
ingredients.reason: Natural coloring from beetroot. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Paprika Extract
ingredients.reason: Extracted from paprika peppers. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Chlorophyll
ingredients.reason: Plant pigment (green color). Extracted from alfalfa, nettles, or grass. Halal.

ingredients.name: Saffron
ingredients.reason: Spice from Crocus sativus flower. Plant-based. Halal. Widely used in Muslim cuisine.

ingredients.name: Beta-Carotene
ingredients.reason: Found in plants (carrots, pumpkins) or produced synthetically. Halal. (Some gelatin-encapsulated forms exist - verify carrier.)

ingredients.name: Tartrazine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic food dye (coal tar derivative). No animal content. Halal. (Health concerns exist but halal status is confirmed.)

ingredients.name: Sunset Yellow
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Brilliant Blue
ingredients.reason: Synthetic dye. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Allura Red
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Indigo Carmine
ingredients.reason: Synthetic dye or from Indigofera plant. Halal.

ingredients.name: Ponceau 4R
ingredients.reason: Synthetic azo dye (not to be confused with cochineal/E120). No animal content. Halal. (Banned in some countries for health reasons.)

ingredients.name: Caramel Color
ingredients.reason: E150c and E150d are produced using ammonium compounds and may contain trace amounts of 4-MEI (a byproduct). E150a (plain caramel) is halal. Some manufacturers may use alcohol in processing. Verify source.

ingredients.name: L-Tryptophan
ingredients.reason: Can be extracted from animal protein or produced synthetically/microbially. Synthetic/microbial L-tryptophan is halal. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Cod Liver Oil
ingredients.reason: From cod fish. All seafood is halal in Islam. Ensure capsules are gelatin-free or halal gelatin.

ingredients.name: Evening Primrose Oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-based oil from evening primrose seeds. Halal. Verify capsule material is halal.

ingredients.name: Glucosamine
ingredients.reason: Commonly derived from shellfish shells. While shellfish is halal, most commercial glucosamine is from non-halal slaughtered animal cartilage. Also, some products are from shellfish but may be processed in facilities with cross-contamination. Plant-based glucosamine is available.

ingredients.name: Chondroitin
ingredients.reason: Typically extracted from bovine or shark cartilage. Bovine source must be from halal-slaughtered animal. Most commercial chondroitin is from non-halal sources. Plant-based alternatives available.

ingredients.name: Maltodextrin
ingredients.reason: Produced from starch (corn, potato, rice, wheat). Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Dextrose
ingredients.reason: Simple sugar from corn starch or other plant sources. Halal.

ingredients.name: Inulin
ingredients.reason: Dietary fiber from chicory root and other plants. Halal.

ingredients.name: Xylitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol from birch bark or corn cobs. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Erythritol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol produced by fermentation. Halal.

ingredients.name: Maltitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol from maltose. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sorbitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol from glucose. Halal. Commonly used in sugar-free foods.

ingredients.name: Mannitol
ingredients.reason: Sugar alcohol from plant sources. Halal.

ingredients.name: Calcium Propionate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative for bread. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sodium Nitrite
ingredients.reason: Synthetic preservative/curing agent. No animal content. Halal. (Health concerns exist, but halal status is confirmed.)

ingredients.name: BHA
ingredients.reason: Synthetic antioxidant. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: BHT
ingredients.reason: Synthetic antioxidant. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Taurine
ingredients.reason: Can be produced synthetically (halal) or extracted from animal sources (often non-halal). Energy drinks often use synthetic taurine. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Lactose
ingredients.reason: Natural sugar from milk. Halal. No animal slaughter involved.

ingredients.name: Lactic Acid
ingredients.reason: Produced by bacterial fermentation of carbohydrates (corn starch). Not from milk despite the name. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lactate
ingredients.reason: Salt of lactic acid. Produced by fermentation. Halal.

ingredients.name: Potassium Chloride
ingredients.reason: Mineral-based salt substitute. Halal.

ingredients.name: Calcium Chloride
ingredients.reason: Mineral salt. Halal.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Chloride
ingredients.reason: Mineral salt. Used in tofu making. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sodium Caseinate (halal)
ingredients.reason: When produced from halal-certified milk and using microbial/vegetarian rennet. Must have halal certification.

ingredients.name: Whey Protein (halal-certified)
ingredients.reason: Whey from halal-certified cheese production using microbial/vegetarian enzymes. Must have halal certification.

ingredients.name: Palm Oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-based oil from palm fruit. Halal. (Environmental/ethical concerns exist but halal status is confirmed.)

ingredients.name: Olive Oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-based. Mentioned in the Quran as a blessed tree (An-Nur 24:35). Halal.

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.reason: Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Shea Butter
ingredients.reason: Plant-based fat from shea tree nuts. Halal.

ingredients.name: Cocoa Butter
ingredients.reason: Plant-based fat from cacao beans. Halal. (Verify chocolate products for other haram ingredients like alcohol or gelatin.)

ingredients.name: Sodium Alginate
ingredients.reason: Extracted from brown seaweed. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Potassium Alginate
ingredients.reason: From brown seaweed. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Alginate
ingredients.reason: From brown seaweed. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Calcium Alginate
ingredients.reason: From brown seaweed. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Alginic Acid
ingredients.reason: From brown seaweed. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Agar-Agar Substitute (Gellan Gum)
ingredients.reason: Produced by bacterial fermentation (Sphingomonas elodea). Halal.

ingredients.name: Konjac
ingredients.reason: From konjac plant (Amorphophallus konjac). Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Tapioca Starch
ingredients.reason: From cassava root. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Cornstarch
ingredients.reason: From corn. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Rice Starch
ingredients.reason: From rice. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Potato Starch
ingredients.reason: From potatoes. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Modified Food Starch
ingredients.reason: Chemically modified starch from corn, potato, or tapioca. Plant-based. Halal. (Rarely, some modified starches may use animal-derived reagents - must verify.)

ingredients.name: Invert Sugar
ingredients.reason: Mixture of glucose and fructose from sucrose. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: High Fructose Corn Syrup
ingredients.reason: From corn starch. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Molasses
ingredients.reason: Byproduct of sugar refining from sugarcane. Plant-based. Halal.

ingredients.name: Date Syrup
ingredients.reason: From dates. Plant-based. Halal. Dates are sunnah food.

ingredients.name: Cholesterol (animal)
ingredients.reason: If derived from non-halal animal sources (pork, non-zabiha). Plant sterols (phytosterols) are halal alternatives.

ingredients.name: Squalene (animal)
ingredients.reason: Can be from shark liver (halal - seafood) or from non-halal animal sources. Olive-derived squalene is halal. Must verify source.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Can be from coconut/palm oil (halal) or from sperm whale (haram). Most commercial cetyl alcohol is plant-based. Verify source.

ingredients.name: Stearyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Can be from plant or animal sources. Plant-based is halal. Verify source.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Palmitate
ingredients.reason: Can be synthetic (halal) or from sperm whale head wax (haram). Most commercial forms are synthetic or plant-based. Verify.

ingredients.name: Isoamyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Derived from fermentation and often contains significant alcohol content. Haram.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Used as a preservative and solvent. Not intoxicating, but may be derived from petroleum or plant sources. Generally considered halal by IFANCA, but some bodies require verification. Check context of use.

ingredients.name: Lanolin Alcohol
ingredients.reason: Derivative of lanolin (from sheep's wool). Generally from live sheep shearing, which is acceptable, but must verify no haram processing aids used.

ingredients.name: Sodium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic surfactant derived from coconut/palm oil or petroleum. Halal.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
ingredients.reason: Synthetic surfactant. Derived from coconut/palm oil or petroleum. Halal.

ingredients.name: Cetyltrimethylammonium Bromide
ingredients.reason: Synthetic compound. Halal.

ingredients.name: Pluronic / Poloxamer
ingredients.reason: Synthetic block copolymer. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: PEG (Polyethylene Glycol)
ingredients.reason: Synthetic polymer. No animal content. Halal.

ingredients.name: Carmellose Sodium
ingredients.reason: Derived from plant cellulose (wood pulp, cotton). Halal.

ingredients.name: Methylcellulose
ingredients.reason: Derived from plant cellulose. Halal.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose
ingredients.reason: Derived from plant cellulose. Halal. Common halal alternative to gelatin capsules.

ingredients.name: Ethylcellulose
ingredients.reason: Derived from plant cellulose. Despite the 'ethyl' in the name, it is not ethanol. Halal.

ingredients.name: Microcrystalline Cellulose
ingredients.reason: Derived from plant cellulose. Halal.

notes: Open Food Facts is a crowdsourced database - data may be incomplete or inaccurate
The halal label is not consistently applied across all products
Ingredients text may contain localized names
For production use, consider caching results
No authentication required for basic queries
Always verify with official halal certification bodies
